description: Per-tenant auto-escalation config consumed by pgr-services EscalationScheduler. defaultSlaByLevel is SLA windows in ms (one per escalation depth); overrides keys by serviceCode for per-complaint-type SLA; maxDepth caps how many times a complaint auto-escalates. enabledByLevel opts in per level. Without a record pgr-services uses its 5-day default SLA (issue #585).

enabledByLevel.description: Optional per-level enablement flags. Same length as defaultSlaByLevel. Missing or empty = all levels enabled (current behaviour). Per @vinothrallapalli-eGov review on PR #692 — keeps the door open for tenants that want escalation only at level N without rewriting the schema.

overrides.description: Per-serviceCode SLA override. Value may be EITHER (a) a plain array of SLA windows (same shape as defaultSlaByLevel, applies to all levels) for backward compatibility, OR (b) an object with explicit `slaByLevel` and optional `enabledByLevel` for per-service-code per-level control.

description: Declares the ORDERED, CONFIGURABLE levels of a complaint-type hierarchy for a tenant. Direct analogue of boundary-service's HierarchyDefinition. One record per (tenantId, hierarchyType). The number/identity of levels is pure data in `levels[]` — add/remove an element to change depth, no schema or code change.

hierarchyType.description: Short uppercase identifier for this hierarchy, e.g. PGR.

levels.description: Ordered top->leaf levels. Exactly one level should set isLeafServiceCode=true; the codes of ComplaintHierarchy rows at that level are the serviceCodes stored on complaints.

description: Single adjacency-list master for the whole complaint-type tree: interior level nodes and leaf sub-types in one table. levelCode matches a ComplaintHierarchyDefinition level; parentCode points at the parent (null at root); path is the materialised path. Leaf rows add department, departments[], slaHours and keywords, and a leaf's code IS the serviceCode stored on a complaint. Replaces the old ClassificationNode + ServiceDefs masters.

code.description: Node code, unique within (hierarchyType, code). For a leaf row this IS the serviceCode stored on a complaint.

department.description: Leaf rows only — the PRIMARY owning department code (the single value backend routing/validation uses). Omitted on interior nodes.

departments.description: Leaf rows only — ALL departments this complaint type routes to (re-expresses the removed ComplaintTypeDepartments master inline). `department` above is the primary of this list. Omitted on interior nodes.

slaHours.description: Leaf rows only — resolution SLA in hours. Omitted on interior nodes.

keywords.description: Leaf rows only — comma-separated search keywords. Omitted on interior nodes.

description: Config-driven notification routing (the 'who'): one row per (businessService, action, toState, audience, channel) declaring that this transition notifies that audience over that channel. Joins 1:1 with NotificationTemplate. Read by pgr-services NotificationRouter.

fromState.description: Documentation/UI only. Runtime matching uses action+toState because the notification consumer reconstructs ServiceRequest from the Kafka record, which carries only the resulting applicationStatus (toState).

action.description: Workflow action, e.g. ASSIGN, REASSIGN, REJECT, RESOLVE, REOPEN, RATE, APPLY.

toState.description: Resulting applicationStatus after the action, e.g. PENDINGATLME. Disambiguates same-action transitions (e.g. RATE -> CLOSEDAFTERRESOLUTION vs CLOSEDAFTERREJECTION).

audience.description: Role code to notify; CITIZEN=the filer; AUTO_ESCALATE/SYSTEM are non-notifiable.

assigneeOnly.description: If true and a named assignee exists, notify only the assignee instead of the whole role pool.

description: Config-driven notification content (the 'what'). One record per (audience, action, toState, channel, locale) holding the message body PGR renders BEFORE publishing to Kafka. audience is the normalized recipient class (CITIZEN, or EMPLOYEE for any employee-type subscriber). Replaces the egov-localization PGR_<ROLE>_<ACTION>_<STATUS>_SMS_MESSAGE keys. Edited via the configurator.

audience.description: Role code to notify; CITIZEN=the filer; AUTO_ESCALATE/SYSTEM are non-notifiable.

body.description: Message body with {placeholder} tokens filled by pgr-services TemplateRenderer before Kafka publish.

placeholders.description: Declared {tokens} this body uses, e.g. id, complaint_type, citizen_name, emp_name, ulb, status, date, download_link, rating, additional_comments.

description: Config-driven provider template mapping (the 'how it's delivered'): one record per (provider, channel, audience, action, toState, locale) mapping a rendered notification to the external provider's pre-registered template and its ordered variables (e.g. a Twilio Content SID for WhatsApp/SMS). Read by novu-bridge to select the templateId and fill variables in order when the provider requires a pre-approved template. Joins to NotificationTemplate by (audience, action, toState, channel, locale).

provider.description: External delivery provider whose template registry this row targets, e.g. twilio.

audience.description: Role code to notify; CITIZEN=the filer; AUTO_ESCALATE/SYSTEM are non-notifiable.

templateId.description: Provider-side template identifier, e.g. a Twilio Content SID (HX...).

templateName.description: Human-readable provider template name, for reference in the configurator.

variables.description: Ordered {token} names substituted into the provider template positionally, e.g. complaint_type, id, date, emp_name.

approvalStatus.description: Provider approval state of the template, e.g. approved, pending, rejected.

description: Feature flag + config for the PGR inbox My/All visibility tabs (Visibility V1, PR #1052 / issue #1020). `enabled: false` (or no record) renders the legacy inbox — no tabs, assigned-to-me radio, open-states default — so per-tenant rollout/rollback is a pure MDMS flip with no frontend redeploy. `version` selects the predicate matrix (v1 role/state, v2 +assignee, v3 +escalation trail) once the server-side resolver (Step 2) lands; the frontend Step 1 only reads `enabled`.

code.description: Fixed discriminator so the master is a singleton per tenant.

version.description: Predicate matrix version (design doc §3). Ignored by FE Step 1.

reporteeDepth.description: Reportee subtree depth for the All tab (server-side resolver, Step 2). Default 1.

jurisdictionScoped.description: AND the jurisdiction axis onto every predicate (Step 2).

serverSide.description: Resolve visibility in pgr-services (/request/inbox/_search, reportee-aware Step-2 resolver) instead of composing it client-side. Requires PGR_VISIBILITY_ENABLED on the deployment. Default false.
